title: Issues when running podman from within VS Code snap sandbox
body:
### Issue Description

If you try to use Podman from within the Snap version of VS Code's integrated terminal, you run into an error like this:

```
podman version
Error: database static dir "/home/rdong8/snap/code/219/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod" does not match our static dir "/home/rdong8/snap/code/232/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod": database configuration mismatch
```

You get this even with zero extensions enabled. This makes it impossible to use dev containers based on Podman through the VS Code Snap, which is my use case for this. However I have found it to work in the following cases:

- If Podman is on a remote host that the Snap VS Code is connected to, it works fine
- If install a new instance of VS Code, ie. VS Code Insiders via Snap, then it seems to work fine

Based on the second point, it seems to be related to how Snap changes the `~/snap/code/<N>` number when it updates applications. Somehow Podman has hardcoded one of these somewhere and it's causing problems. `podman system reset` does not help. 

Wasn't sure whether to report this to Podman, Snap or VS Code but I felt like you guys might have the most insight on how I can work around this here. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install the VS Code Snap
2. Run `podman version` in the integrated terminal. Issue may only surface after you update the Snap at least once


### Describe the results you received

```
Error: database static dir "/home/rdong8/snap/code/219/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod" does not match our static dir "/home/rdong8/snap/code/232/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod": database configuration mismatch
```

### Describe the results you expected

Command works fine

### Additional information

comments.body: could you please also try with v5.8.2 released yesterday? There's a fedora 43 update in testing https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2026-75c2b7868a .
I've just checked and it still happens

> `podman system reset` does not help.

Just to confirm, did you run this from inside the VS Code snap terminal?
No, if I do that I get the same error. I had to run it on my system terminal.
@mheon as the resident db dude, care to speculate ?

It appears that VSCode + Snap is trying to spawn Podman in a different directory each time it runs. Our files are still intact (at least, I assume so, based on the fact that we found the database) but they aren't where we expect them to be, so we're throwing an error - we store full paths in the database, not relative ones, so the change in directory would break things if we continued. A `podman system reset` will clear the error, at the cost of getting rid of all containers/pods/images/volumes.

A proper fix would require a major rework of how we store things in the database, to use only paths relative to the c/storage root, not absolute paths. We've discussed this in the past but it's never been high enough priority to implement.
`podman system reset` does not clear the error for me. I also tried uninstalling the podman that comes with fedora and installing it through homebrew, still get the same error.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
